reforms.description: The government has implemented several programmes to support new high technology start-ups through seed capital and subsidised loan programmes, with DM 13 billion allocated for 1998 and 1999 to assist approximately 30,000 start-ups, including guarantees for equity participation financed by development banks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting start-ups fosters innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programmes have been put into action to support start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant funding and support for start-ups, but not a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has introduced subsidised loan programmes through one of the public banks... DM 13 billion has been made available in both 1998 and 1999.

reforms.description: The government has taken steps to improve the performance of its research centres by encouraging them to enhance contacts with industry and retain revenues from such activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing industry contacts improves the commercialization of research outputs.
reforms.status_evidence: The steps have been actively taken to enhance research centre performance.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant efforts to improve research centre performance, but not a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The government has taken steps to improve the performance of its research centres.

reforms.description: The German government has initiated reforms to streamline the approval process for opening plants and workshops, created a new lending programme with favourable terms to finance job creation projects, and deepened financial markets to foster venture capital, improving access to equity capital for companies.
reforms.component_name: Approval Process and Financial Market Reforms
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining processes and improving access to finance enhances innovation and economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted to support innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant reforms to support innovation, but not a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: A new lending programme with favourable terms has been created by the federal government to help finance the materials and capital costs of job creation projects.

reforms.description: A new bankruptcy code is set to be introduced in 1999, broadening the grounds for filing bankruptcy claims and allowing directors to re-enter economic life more quickly, which aims to support entrepreneurship.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating quicker re-entry into business supports entrepreneurial activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The introduction of the new bankruptcy code has been officially announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant reform to support entrepreneurship, but not a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: A new code is to be introduced at the beginning of 1999 which will define the grounds for filing bankruptcy claims in broader terms than in the past.

reforms.description: The government aims to increase participation in active labour market programmes by 200,000 persons, with a budget increase of DM 5 billion to DM 40 billion.
reforms.component_name: Expansion of Active Labour Market Programmes
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing participation in labour market programmes enhances the skills and employability of the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme expansion has been actively implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant increase in funding and participation, but not a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Active labour market schemes are being expanded again in 1998, the government aiming for participation in the programmes to increase by some 200,000 persons.
